responsibilities: Architect end-to-end autonomy stacks that integrate perception, localization, prediction, planning, and control modules into a cohesive system
Design decision-making and behavior planning logic that enables autonomous systems to safely navigate complex, dynamic environments
Develop and validate finite state machines and behavior trees to govern high-level autonomous system mode transitions and fallback behaviors
Build and run large-scale software-in-the-loop and simulation testing campaigns to validate autonomy stack performance before field deployment
Collaborate with perception, planning, and controls teams to define system-level interfaces and data contracts across the autonomy stack
Conduct root-cause analysis of autonomy system failures using simulation replay and field log data to drive design improvements
Define and track system-level safety requirements and verification criteria to support functional safety and regulatory compliance
Lead system integration efforts across hardware, software, and sensor subsystems to deliver reliable autonomous platform behavior
Develop digital twin and simulation environments to accelerate development and reduce reliance on costly physical testing
